title: Diameter of Binary Tree

hints: The diameter of a tree is the longest path between any two nodes.
For each node, the diameter passing through it is: height of left subtree + height of right subtree + 2.
Use a recursive approach to calculate heights of subtrees.
While calculating height, also track the maximum diameter found so far.
Return -1 for null nodes (so height of leaf nodes becomes 0).
The diameter might pass through the root, or it might be entirely in a subtree.
Keep updating the maximum diameter as you traverse the tree.
